title: gonolobus hystrix
intro: Gonolobus hystrix, also known as the porcupine gourd or prickly poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues. Gonolobus hystrix can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal resource in traditional medicine.
